steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank must logically complete the argument set up by “suggesting that ______”. The structure is: (1) many studies found a positive association between dissolved organic carbon and mercury; (2) Fournier found no association in wastewater-disturbed waters, prompting a hypothesis that the association only exists in undisturbed waters; (3) Lindgren found the association does exist in urban-development-disturbed waters — matching the original studies. The blank must capture what Lindgren's result implies about the earlier hypothesis.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "the effects of wastewater on the association between levels of dissolved organic carbon and mercury should not be taken as indicative of the effects of every type of human disturbance.": Lindgren's result shows that urban disturbance preserves the association while wastewater breaks it — so wastewater is not representative of all human disturbances. This follows directly. ✓
  • "Fournier, Rousseau, and colleagues' study used different methods to measure the concentration of mercury in fresh water than Lindgren and colleagues' study did.": No methodological difference is mentioned in the passage. ✗
  • "disturbances linked to wastewater affect significantly more bodies of fresh water than disturbances linked to urban development do.": The passage says nothing about the relative scale of each type of disturbance. ✗
  • "levels of dissolved organic carbon and mercury are both much higher in bodies of fresh water impacted by wastewater than they are in bodies of fresh water disturbed by urban development.": The passage describes whether the association exists, not the absolute levels of either substance. ✗
That matches the correct answer — A is correct.
